behavior_summary: When the player uses the 'U8P_FISHBONZ::look' (inspect) action on the FISHBONZ entity, the script emits a single short descriptive bark: "fish bones".
After displaying the bark the process yields/suspends, indicating the U8P_FISHBONZ::look handler ends immediately after the message.
No branching, menus, parameters, external calls, or global flag reads/writes are present — this is a minimal descriptive U8P_FISHBONZ::look handler.
notable_control_flow: Execution sequence: display Bark("fish bones") -> suspend/yield the process.
No conditionals or menu logic; two unknown/decompiler remnants are present but do not alter the observable behavior.
open_questions: Are the Unknown nodes purely decompiler remnants or do they represent invisible engine bookkeeping? (low priority — behavior is clear.)
Is the bark text localized via an external lookup key in the engine, or embedded literally as shown? (inference only).

main_function.name: U8P_FISHBONZ::look
main_function.purpose: Provide the short description when the player inspects the FISHBONZ entity by showing a single bark and ending the inspection process.
entry_conditions: Triggered by the player's U8P_FISHBONZ::look/inspect action on the entity (process type 0x0208).
Inferred: invoked whenever the engine routes a 'U8P_FISHBONZ::look' event to this NPC/object; no additional runtime guards present.
